commit.message:
fix: make ValidationError inherit from ValueError for backward compatibility

Integration tests for delegation chain depth use pytest.raises(ValueError).
Task 9.3 changed the chain_depth_exceeded raise from ValueError to
ValidationError to carry structured detail (chain_depth_remaining,
effective_max_chain_depth). Making ValidationError a subclass of both
AgentAuthError and ValueError satisfies both: the API layer gets the
structured detail dict, and callers catching ValueError still work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop evicting message-level cache breakpoints for Anthropic Messages API (#4410)

* fix: stop evicting message-level cache breakpoints in addToolsAndSystemCacheControl

Message-level cache_control entries are more valuable than tools/system
breakpoints for long conversations because they implicitly cache the
tools+system prefix (Anthropic cache hierarchy: tools → system → messages)
and maintain the 20-block lookback chain needed for cache hits in long
agent sessions.

Previously, the function would evict earliest message breakpoints to make
room for tools+system breakpoints, breaking cache coverage for
claude-opus-4.6-1m conversations with many tool calls.

Now tools+system breakpoints are only added when spare slots exist
(existing < 4), never evicting message breakpoints.

commit.message:
fix: Windows supervisor dies on SSH session exit (#7)

* fix: add CREATE_BREAKAWAY_FROM_JOB for Windows session detachment

On Windows, when bgtask is launched via SSH (e.g. OpenSSH server),
the supervisor process was killed when the SSH session ended. This
happened because the SSH server places child processes in a Job Object,
and when the session closes, all processes in the job are terminated.

Adding CREATE_BREAKAWAY_FROM_JOB to the process creation flags allows
the supervisor to break out of the session's job object, surviving
SSH disconnects.

If the parent job doesn't allow breakaway (ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED), fall
back to the previous behavior without the flag — the supervisor will
start but may not survive session exit.

Also replaced raw hex constants with named constants from x/sys/windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract detach helper and surface breakaway fallback warning

- Extract newDetachedCmd() to deduplicate command setup between main
  and fallback paths in Detach (Windows)
- Return bool from Detach indicating breakaway failure so callers can
  warn that the supervisor may not survive session exit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix commit snapshot cloning race

Restore IndexWriter.prepareCommitInternal commit snapshot parity with
upstream Java. The Kotlin port was eagerly cloning segmentInfos before
entering the full flush and IndexWriter locked snapshot section, which
left SegmentInfos.clone iterating a live mutable list during concurrent
commit activity and occasionally throwing ConcurrentModificationException.

Initialize toCommit as null, take the segmentInfos clone only inside the
existing full flush plus IndexWriter lock protected snapshot block, and
use checkNotNull when the prepared snapshot is consumed later in commit
processing. This keeps the commit snapshot stable and matches Lucene's
lock ordering.

Validation:
- focused JVM compile/test rerun passed after the fix
- user reran the scenario 200 times locally without reproducing the failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: heal @github/copilot/definitions/ when missing after --install-links (#22)

* fix: heal @github/copilot/definitions/ when missing after --install-links

npm install -g --install-links uses a stricter tar extractor that fails to
create parent directories before writing files into them, leaving
node_modules/@github/copilot/definitions/ absent and causing
TAR_ENTRY_ERROR ENOENT warnings.

The postinstall script now detects the missing directory and re-installs
@github/copilot at the exact pinned version using --no-install-links,

commit.message:
feat: extract Discord embed content in message link resolution (#18)

Sonarr and other bot integrations send notifications as Discord embeds
(rich objects with title, description, fields, footer) rather than plain
msg.content. The previous implementation fell back to '(no text content)'
for these messages, making linked context useless.

Changes:
- Add formatEmbeds() helper in resolveMessageLinks.ts that serialises
  msg.embeds[] into quoted plain-text lines (author, title, description,
  fields, footer)
- Use flatMap().split('\n') to ensure multiline field values are each
  individually prefixed with '>' (caught by adversarial review)
- Body assembly now combines msg.content + embed blocks; falls back to
  '(no text content)' only when both are empty
- Multiple embeds separated by '> ---' divider
